name: Digest and analyse YouTube videos with Apify and Gemini Pro

parameters.content:
## YouTube Shorts Automation v7.0
### @adamfreelances

**APIFY YOUTUBE APPROACH**

Simplified workflow:
1. Form takes YouTube URL
2. Apify downloads video
3. Apify gets transcript
4. Gemini detects meaningful moments
5. Returns time ranges + descriptions

**Output per moment:**
- Start/end timestamps
- App name
- Descriptive action

## Stage 1: YouTube URL Input

**Trigger:** Form submission

1. User submits YouTube URL
2. Apify downloads video
3. Apify extracts transcript
4. Combine data for processing

## Stage 2A: Transcript

Apify YouTube Transcript:
- Gets transcript directly from YT
- No audio processing needed
- Fast, reliable transcription

Returns full transcript text.

parameters.formDescription: Submit a YouTube video URL for automated shorts creation

parameters.content:
## Stage 2B: Visual Analysis

**KEY ACTION DETECTION v2**

Gemini watches the whole video and
identifies MEANINGFUL moments:

- What the user accomplished
- Time ranges (start в†’ end)
- Descriptive actions, not clicks

Examples:
- "User created a Code node and
   connected it to the trigger"
- "User typed a prompt into Claude
   asking for API integration help"

values.content:
=You are a video content analyzer for @adamfreelances, a technical YouTube channel focused on AI development, n8n automation, and building custom CRM systems.

Your job is to analyze video transcripts to extract comprehensive metadata for:
- Content repurposing
- SEO optimization
- Video understanding

Always maintain the brand voice: technical, educational, anti-guru (no hype, real implementation focus).

Respond ONLY with valid JSON matching the required schema. No markdown, no explanations, just the JSON object.

=Analyze this video content and extract key metadata.

## TRANSCRIPT
{{ $json.transcript_text }}

## KEY MOMENTS (Visual Analysis)
These are the on-screen actions detected by visual analysis:
{{ $json.key_moments.toJsonString() }}

## VIDEO METADATA
- Duration: {{ $json.duration }} seconds
- Segment count: {{ $json.segments.length }}
- Video name: {{ $json.video_name }}
- Video Description: {{ $('Set Video Data').item.json.description }}

## INSTRUCTIONS
1. Divide the content into 3-8 logical sections based on topic changes
2. Extract a comprehensive video overview with summary, key takeaways, and SEO data
3. Identify the best sections that could work as standalone YouTube Shorts (15-60 seconds)
4. List all tools, frameworks, and technologies mentioned
5. Generate suggested titles and SEO keywords

Return the structured JSON analysis.

parameters.text:
=Analyze this ENTIRE video from start to finish and find 3-second B-roll clips with maximum visual movement. You MUST scan the complete video duration, not just the intro.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
PERSON VISIBILITY & WEBCAM OVERLAY DETECTION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

This prompt handles TWO distinct scenarios:

SCENARIO A: FULL-FRAME PERSON (person_visible: true, has_usable_screen_content: false)
- Talking head fills most/all of the frame
- No significant screen recording content behind them
- These clips are NOT usable for B-roll
- Mark as: person_visible: true, has_usable_screen_content: false

SCENARIO B: SCREEN RECORDING WITH WEBCAM OVERLAY (person_visible: true, has_usable_screen_content: true)
- Main content is screen recording (code editor, browser, app UI, etc.)
- Small webcam overlay shows person's face in a corner
- The screen content IS usable if we crop out the webcam region
- Mark as: person_visible: true, has_usable_screen_content: true
- MUST provide webcam_region and safe_crop_zone data

SCENARIO C: CLEAN SCREEN RECORDING (person_visible: false)
- Pure screen recording with no person visible anywhere
- Ideal B-roll - no cropping needed
- Mark as: person_visible: false, has_usable_screen_content: true

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
5-SECOND BUFFER CHECK (CRITICAL FOR ALL SCENARIOS)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

You are extracting 3-second clips, but you MUST verify a 5-SECOND WINDOW for consistency.
For a clip timestamped start to end, check this EXPANDED range:

BUFFER START = start minus 1 second
BUFFER END = end plus 1 second

Check these 5 points within the 5-second buffer zone:

1. BUFFER START (-1 sec before clip starts)
2. CLIP START (actual start of the 3-sec clip)
3. CLIP MIDDLE (1.5 seconds into the clip)
4. CLIP END (actual end of the 3-sec clip)
5. BUFFER END (+1 sec after clip ends)

Example: For a clip at 0:10-0:13, check: 0:09, 0:10, 0:11.5, 0:13, 0:14

For SCENARIO B (webcam overlay), verify the webcam stays in the SAME position across all 5 checkpoints.
If the webcam moves or disappears mid-clip, the safe_crop_zone is invalid.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
вљ пёЏ  PERSON DETECTION ACCURACY - READ CAREFULLY  вљ пёЏ
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

WHAT COUNTS AS "PERSON VISIBLE" - BE EXTREMELY STRICT:
вњ— Full face on screen = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Partial face (forehead, chin, cheek, ear) = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Side profile of face = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Back of head = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Webcam overlay in corner (ANY size, even tiny) = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Hands typing on physical keyboard = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Arms or shoulders visible = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Body/torso visible = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Reflection of person in screen = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Picture-in-picture with person = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Blurred/transitioning person (fading in/out) = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Person visible for even a SINGLE FRAME within buffer = PERSON VISIBLE
вњ— Silhouette or shadow of person = PERSON VISIBLE

ONLY mark person_visible: false when you are 100% CERTAIN:
вњ“ Pure screen recording with only cursor/typing on screen
вњ“ Software UI with absolutely no human elements
вњ“ Animated graphics/slides with no person
вњ“ Terminal/code editor with no webcam overlay anywhere
вњ“ Browser/app interface with no picture-in-picture

WHEN IN DOUBT, MARK person_visible: true

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
WEBCAM REGION DETECTION (FOR SCENARIO B ONLY)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

When a webcam overlay is present over usable screen content, you MUST:

1. IDENTIFY WEBCAM CORNER POSITION
   - "top-left", "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right", or "custom"
   
2. MEASURE WEBCAM BOUNDING BOX (normalized 0-1 coordinates)
   All values are percentages of the total frame expressed as decimals (0.0 to 1.0)
   
   webcam_region: {
     x_min: [left edge, 0.0 = left side of frame],
     y_min: [top edge, 0.0 = top of frame],
     x_max: [right edge, 1.0 = right side of frame],
     y_max: [bottom edge, 1.0 = bottom of frame]
   }

3. CALCULATE SAFE CROP ZONE
   The largest RECTANGULAR region that completely EXCLUDES the webcam.
   This will be used by Creatomate to render only the clean portion.
   
   For corner webcams, the safe zone is typically the OPPOSITE strip:
   - Webcam bottom-right в†’ safe zone is everything ABOVE or to the LEFT
   - Webcam top-left в†’ safe zone is everything BELOW or to the RIGHT
   
   Choose the crop that preserves the MOST screen content while fully excluding the webcam.

4. ADD PADDING BUFFER
   Add 2% padding around the webcam region to ensure no face pixels leak through.
   If webcam_region.x_min = 0.75, use 0.73 for safe_crop_zone.x_max

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
SAFE CROP ZONE CALCULATION EXAMPLES
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

EXAMPLE 1: Webcam in BOTTOM-RIGHT corner
Frame: 1920x1080, Webcam occupies bottom-right ~25% width, ~30% height

webcam_region: {
  x_min: 0.75,  // starts at 75% from left
  y_min: 0.70,  // starts at 70% from top
  x_max: 1.0,   // extends to right edge
  y_max: 1.0    // extends to bottom edge
}

OPTION A - Crop to TOP portion (preserves full width):
safe_crop_zone: {
  x_min: 0.0,
  y_min: 0.0,
  x_max: 1.0,
  y_max: 0.68   // 2% padding above webcam
}
Result: Full 1920px width, top 68% of height (734px)

OPTION B - Crop to LEFT portion (preserves full height):
safe_crop_zone: {
  x_min: 0.0,
  y_min: 0.0,
  x_max: 0.73,  // 2% padding left of webcam
  y_max: 1.0
}
Result: Left 73% of width (1402px), full 1080px height

Choose based on which preserves more important content. Generally prefer OPTION A for horizontal screen recordings.


EXAMPLE 2: Webcam in TOP-LEFT corner
Frame: 1920x1080, Webcam occupies top-left ~20% width, ~25% height

webcam_region: {
  x_min: 0.0,
  y_min: 0.0,
  x_max: 0.20,
  y_max: 0.25
}

safe_crop_zone: {
  x_min: 0.0,
  y_min: 0.27,  // 2% padding below webcam
  x_max: 1.0,
  y_max: 1.0
}
Result: Full width, bottom 73% of height


EXAMPLE 3: Webcam in BOTTOM-LEFT corner (circular overlay)
Frame: 1920x1080, Circular webcam overlay

webcam_region: {
  x_min: 0.02,
  y_min: 0.65,
  x_max: 0.22,
  y_max: 0.98
}

safe_crop_zone: {
  x_min: 0.0,
  y_min: 0.0,
  x_max: 1.0,
  y_max: 0.63
}
Result: Full width, top 63% of height

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
QUANTITY REQUIREMENTS
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

MAXIMIZE CLIPS - Extract as many qualifying B-roll clips as possible
- For videos under 10 minutes: Find 50-100 clips
- For videos 10-20 minutes: Find 100-150 clips
- For videos 20+ minutes: Find 150-250+ clips

3-SECOND CLIPS = MORE CLIPS - Shorter duration means you can capture more moments

If you're finding fewer than 50, LOWER your threshold - include clips with moderate movement

Better to include a borderline clip than miss good B-roll

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
FULL VIDEO ANALYSIS REQUIREMENT
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Analyze from 0:00 to the very end

- Intros often have more animation, but the BODY of the video contains valuable B-roll too
- Specifically look for clips in the MIDDLE and LATER portions (after 2:00)
- Aim for clips distributed EVENLY across the video timeline, not clustered at the start
- Break the video into quarters and find clips in EACH quarter
- LEAVE NO GAPS - If there's 30+ seconds without a clip, look harder at that section

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
WHAT MAKES GREAT B-ROLL (3 seconds)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

HIGH PRIORITY - Lots of visual movement:
- Rapid typing with code appearing on screen
- Dragging and dropping nodes/elements
- Drawing connections between nodes
- UI elements animating or expanding
- Terminal output scrolling rapidly
- Files/folders being created or moved
- Cursor moving purposefully across screen
- Workflow executing with visible progress
- Code being highlighted or selected
- Browser navigation with page loads
- Switching between applications
- Form filling with visible text entry
- API responses populating on screen
- Clicking through menus or dropdowns
- Selecting options or checkboxes
- Copy/paste actions with visible results

MEDIUM PRIORITY - Include to maximize coverage:
- Moderate scrolling through code or content
- Clicking buttons with visible state changes
- Tab switching in browser or IDE
- Expanding/collapsing sections
- Cursor selection highlighting text
- Loading spinners with content appearing after
- Mouse moving between elements
- Tooltips or hover states appearing

SKIP - Not visually interesting:
- Completely static screens (2+ seconds no movement)
- Still slides or title cards held for reading
- Mouse sitting idle

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
OUTPUT FORMAT - CREATOMATE-READY JSON
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
FIELD SCHEMA - ALL FIELDS REQUIRED
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

CORE TIMING FIELDS:
| Field | Type | Format | Example |
|-------|------|--------|---------|
| clip_id | string | "clip_NNN" | "clip_001" |
| start | string | "M:SS" or "MM:SS" | "0:05", "12:34" |
| end | string | "M:SS" or "MM:SS" | "0:08", "12:37" |
| start_seconds | integer | seconds from 0:00 | 5, 754 |
| end_seconds | integer | start_seconds + 3 | 8, 757 |

CONTENT DESCRIPTION FIELDS:
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| app | string | Application or platform shown on screen |
| action | string | What is visually happening (be specific about movement) |
| screen_description | string | Brief description of what the screen looks like |

PERSON DETECTION FIELDS:
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| person_visible | boolean | true if ANY person element visible at ANY buffer checkpoint |
| has_usable_screen_content | boolean | true if main content is screen recording (even with webcam overlay) |
| buffer_check | object | Per-timestamp verification with status for each of 5 checkpoints |

WEBCAM REGION FIELDS (null if person_visible is false OR has_usable_screen_content is false):
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| webcam_region.position | string | "top-left", "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right", "custom" |
| webcam_region.x_min | float | Left edge as 0-1 decimal |
| webcam_region.y_min | float | Top edge as 0-1 decimal |
| webcam_region.x_max | float | Right edge as 0-1 decimal |
| webcam_region.y_max | float | Bottom edge as 0-1 decimal |
| webcam_region.shape | string | "rectangle", "circle", "rounded" |
| webcam_region.approximate_size_percent | integer | Rough % of frame the webcam occupies |

SAFE CROP ZONE FIELDS (null if no usable crop possible):
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| safe_crop_zone.x_min | float | Left edge of safe area as 0-1 decimal |
| safe_crop_zone.y_min | float | Top edge of safe area as 0-1 decimal |
| safe_crop_zone.x_max | float | Right edge of safe area as 0-1 decimal |
| safe_crop_zone.y_max | float | Bottom edge of safe area as 0-1 decimal |
| safe_crop_zone.strategy | string | "crop_above_webcam", "crop_below_webcam", "crop_left_of_webcam", "crop_right_of_webcam" |
| safe_crop_zone.content_preserved_percent | integer | % of original frame preserved after crop |

CREATOMATE CROP FIELDS (null if no crop needed or not usable):
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| creatomate_crop.source_x | string | Starting X position as percentage (e.g., "0%", "25%") |
| creatomate_crop.source_y | string | Starting Y position as percentage (e.g., "0%", "30%") |
| creatomate_crop.source_width | string | Width to extract as percentage (e.g., "100%", "75%") |
| creatomate_crop.source_height | string | Height to extract as percentage (e.g., "70%", "100%") |
| creatomate_crop.fit | string | Always "fill" for cropped content |
| creatomate_crop.clip | boolean | Always true for cropped content |

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
CREATOMATE INTEGRATION NOTES
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

The creatomate_crop object is designed to map directly to Creatomate's composition element.

For a clip with webcam in bottom-right (safe zone is top 70%):
{
  "creatomate_crop": {
    "source_x": "0%",      // Start from left edge
    "source_y": "0%",      // Start from top edge
    "source_width": "100%", // Full width
    "source_height": "70%", // Only top 70%
    "fit": "fill",
    "clip": true
  }
}

This translates to Creatomate RenderScript as:
{
  "type": "composition",
  "width": 1080,  // Your target output width
  "height": 1920, // Your target output height
  "elements": [{
    "type": "video",
    "source": "VIDEO_URL",
    "trim_start": start_seconds,
    "trim_duration": 3,
    "x": "50%",
    "y": "50%",
    "width": "100%",
    "height": "142.86%",  // 100 / 0.70 = ~143% to scale up the cropped region
    "y_anchor": "0%",     // Anchor to top to show the top 70%
    "fit": "fill",
    "clip": true
  }]
}

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
STRICT RESPONSE RULES
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

- START WITH [ - First character of your response must be [
- END WITH ] - Last character of your response must be ]
- NO TEXT BEFORE - No "Here are the clips:" or any introduction
- NO TEXT AFTER - No "I found X clips" or any summary
- NO MARKDOWN - Do not wrap in ```json``` code blocks
- VALID JSON ONLY - Response must parse with JSON.parse()

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
CLIP EXTRACTION RULES
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

1. MAXIMIZE CLIPS - Extract as many as possible. Fill the context window.

2. STRICT 3-SECOND CLIPS - Every clip must be exactly 3 seconds (end_seconds = start_seconds + 3)

3. 5-SECOND BUFFER CHECK - Verify each of the 5 timestamps individually

4. WEBCAM CONSISTENCY - For clips with webcam overlay, verify webcam position is stable across all 5 buffer checkpoints

5. FULL VIDEO COVERAGE - Include clips from intro, middle, AND end sections proportionally

6. MOVEMENT PRIORITY - Prioritize clips where things are visually happening

7. ONE ACTION PER CLIP - Each clip = single focused activity

8. DESCRIPTIVE ACTIONS - Describe what's visually happening, not what's being discussed

9. NO OVERLAPPING - Clips should not overlap. Minimum 1 second gap between clips.

10. CONSECUTIVE OK - Back-to-back clips from the same screen are fine if they capture different actions

11. INCLUDE WEBCAM OVERLAYS - Clips with webcam overlays ARE usable if has_usable_screen_content is true. Provide the crop data.

12. SKIP TALKING HEAD ONLY - Only skip clips where the entire frame is the person with no screen content behind them.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
DISTRIBUTION CHECK
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Before finalizing, verify your clips are distributed across the video:

- First quarter (0% - 25%): Should have ~25% of clips
- Second quarter (25% - 50%): Should have ~25% of clips
- Third quarter (50% - 75%): Should have ~25% of clips
- Final quarter (75% - 100%): Should have ~25% of clips

If any quarter has less than 15% of total clips, go back and find more moments in that section.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

вќЊ "The screen recording started so it must be clean" - WRONG, check each timestamp
вќЊ "I didn't see a face at the start so the whole clip is fine" - WRONG, check ALL 5 points
вќЊ "It's mostly screen recording" - WRONG, detect webcam overlays and provide crop data
вќЊ "The webcam overlay is small/in the corner" - STILL requires webcam_region and safe_crop_zone
вќЊ "The person is blurry/transitioning" - WRONG, still counts as person visible
вќЊ "I checked nearby and it was clean" - WRONG, check the EXACT timestamps listed
вќЊ Skipping clips just because they have webcam overlay - WRONG, these ARE usable with cropping
вќЊ Setting has_usable_screen_content to false when there's a webcam over screen recording - WRONG, the screen content IS usable
вќЊ Providing inconsistent webcam positions across buffer checkpoints
